title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
Ellery Queen discovers that his telegram to Sheriff Redden was never delivered because the sheriff and Mayor Matt Hollis left for a week-long fishing trip in the Ohio River. The caretaker of the office had signed and shelved the telegram. Driven by fear, Ellery drives his Duesenberg to a remote location, carrying a loaded semi-automatic pistol. He ascends a path toward a clearing, noting the silence of the forest. Upon reaching a fence, he observes footprints in the damp earth and finds that a door is slightly ajar. He enters the building, encountering a "terrible sight" inside, and closes the heavy door behind him.

Returning to the town hall, Ellery encounters a doorman who provides him with a note left by a tall, gangly man described as looking like "old Abe Lincoln." The note, signed by Yadley, explains that Yadley traveled to the area via private plane to find Vane and Isam. Yadley reports seeing a limping man, presumably Crozack, who rented a car at Yellow Bay and traveled to a hotel in Stowellville, Ohio. Yadley instructs Ellery to go to Stowellville immediately. Ellery realizes the note was left by Yadley, not Abraham Lincoln, and instructs the doorman to give the note to incoming officials from New York, specifically Eames and Vance. After giving the doorman a hundred-dollar bill, Ellery speeds away.

Meanwhile, Officer Wain and District Attorney Esam arrive in Bradwood with a representative from the U.S. Attorney's office, escorting Percy and Elizabeth Rein. After receiving a report from Professor Adler, the officials realize they must pursue the suspects.
